{"content": "   501|### Integration Strategy
   502|
   503|Vesta v0 should be plugin-first:
   504|
   505|- `ledger_append` and related runtime tools live in a plugin/tool layer.
   506|- Run directory creation, artifact manifests, raw payload references, and
   507|  worker-state recording live outside core transcript storage.
   508|- Existing `pre_tool_call`, `post_tool_call`, `pre_llm_call`, `post_llm_call`,
   509|  `on_session_start`, and `on_session_end` hooks should be reused where they
   510|  are enough.
   511|- Direct core patches should be narrow lifecycle hooks where Hermes currently
   512|  has no clean extension point.
   513|
   514|The likely first missing lifecycle point is compaction/resume. Hermes already
   515|rotates session IDs during compression and informs the active context engine,
   516|but Vesta needs a ledger checkpoint and resume packet at that boundary. Prefer
   517|adding a small upstreamable hook around compression over replacing the entire
   518|context engine or moving Vesta state into Hermes' SQLite transcript tables.
   519|
   520|`run_id` is not the same as Hermes `session_id`. Hermes may rotate the
   521|`session_id` on compression. Vesta should keep one stable `run_id` and record
   522|the active Hermes session lineage inside `run.md` or `ledger.md`.
   523|
   524|### Run Storage
   525|
   526|Borrow Hermes' profile-aware path convention:
   527|
   528|- use `get_hermes_home()` as the only root resolver;
   529|- place Vesta state under the active Hermes profile, not under the OS home
   530|  directly;
   531|- use a module-level base path, analogous to `CHECKPOINT_BASE =
   532|  get_hermes_home() / "checkpoints"`;
   533|- group runs by a deterministic normalized workspace hash, analogous to
   534|  checkpoint project hashes;
   535|- keep a human-readable `workspace.md` or `run.md` next to hashed directories so
   536|  users can inspect paths without decoding hashes.
   537|
   538|Recommended v0 shape:
   539|
   540|```text
   541|{get_hermes_home()}/vesta/
   542|  workspaces/
   543|    <workspace_hash>/
   544|      workspace.md
   545|      runs/
   546|        <run_id>/
   547|          run.md
   548|          ledger.md
   549|          resume-packet.md
   550|          artifact-manifest.md
   551|          raw/
   552|          finalization.md
   553|```
   554|
   555|This follows upstream Hermes behavior closely: persistent state belongs under
   556|the active profile, repo working directories stay clean by default, and path
   557|display should use `display_hermes_home()` in user-facing messages.
   558|
   559|### Worker Model Lane
   560|
   561|Hermes already has the right primitive for v0 worker routing:
   562|
   563|- `delegation.model`;
   564|- `delegation.provider`;
   565|- `delegation.base_url`;
   566|- `delegation.api_key`;
   567|- `delegation.api_mode`;
   568|- `delegation.reasoning_effort`;
   569|- `delegation.max_iterations`;
   570|- `delegation.child_timeout_seconds`;
   571|- `delegation.max_concurrent_children`;
   572|- `delegation.max_spawn_depth`.
   573|
   574|Vesta should borrow this instead of inventing a separate model-router surface in
   575|v0. A run may declare a worker lane in `run.md`, and each
   576|`worker_state` entry should record:
   577|
   578|- parent model/provider;
   579|- worker model/provider/base URL class, without secrets;
   580|- assigned objective;
   581|- output contract;
   582|- artifact paths;
   583|- status/failure;
   584|- reason for delegation;
   585|- cache rationale when relevant.
   586|
   587|This is important for local llama.cpp-style use. A dense main model can keep a
   588|stable prompt/cache while bounded subtasks run in separate child contexts on a
   589|faster adjacent model, such as an MoE worker. The benefit is not only cost; it
   590|also avoids forcing the main model to ingest side-task context and reprocess a
   591|large cached prefix.
   592|
   593|v0 should use explicit or config-driven worker routing. The model may call
   594|Hermes `delegate_task` for bounded side tasks, and a run/eval contract may
   595|declare the worker lane, but Vesta should not auto-route every turn between
   596|models. Automatic model routing can come later as a small deterministic policy,
   597|after the ledger can show which workers were spawned, why, on which model, and
   598|what they produced.
   599|
   600|## Prompt Cache Constraint
   601|
   602|Ledger behavior should be encouraged by stable, resident runtime instructions,
   603|not intermittent prompt injections. The cached prompt prefix should stay as
   604|stable as possible.
   605|
   606|Recommended v0 mechanics:
   607|
   608|- one small resident "ledger protocol" in the system/runtime prompt;
   609|- stable tool descriptions for ledger/read/write behavior;
   610|- no mid-session add/remove of ledger tools or ledger instructions;
   611|- dynamic state kept in `ledger.md`, not injected into every turn;
   612|- compaction/resume injects only a short stable-shaped resume packet with paths
   613|  and next action;
   614|- warnings/nudges should be rare and late in the prompt, not early-prefix churn.
   615|
   616|Do not implement periodic soft reminders in v0. "Soft" ledger behavior means
   617|resident static instructions and stable tool descriptions, not dynamic nudges
   618|every few turns. Dynamic prompts risk breaking llama.cpp prompt-cache similarity
   619|and forcing expensive full reprocessing.
   620|
   621|Hard enforcement should happen only at stable boundaries:
   622|
   623|- before compaction;
   624|- before final response;
   625|- after explicit user decision;
   626|- after artifact write;
   627|- after failed command/test;
   628|- after worker completion/failure;
   629|- when finalization sees unresolved contradictions;
   630|- when finalization sees unsupported high-materiality claims.
   631|
   632|When a hard gate finds missing ledger state, it should request a repair turn
   633|first. The model appends the required entry via `ledger_append`, then continues.
   634|Only block if the repair turn fails. Do not silently auto-append semantic ledger
   635|entries in runtime code; the model owns the meaning.
   636|
   637|Gate bands:
   638|
   639|1. Core flow: compaction, final response, explicit user decision, artifact write.
   640|2. Quality/failure flow: failed command/test, worker completion/failure,
   641|   unresolved contradictions, unsupported high-materiality claims.
   642|
   643|Artifact gates apply to declared durable outputs, not every file edit. Vesta is
   644|not only a coding agent; artifacts are outputs a human may later rely on. A
   645|source-code edit is an artifact only when the run declares it as a deliverable
   646|or records it as part of a patch/diff/result package.
   647|
   648|Declared artifacts use hybrid ownership:
   649|
   650|- runtime declares artifacts from commitments, output contracts, and known run
   651|  paths;
   652|- the model declares semantic artifacts through `ledger_append`;
   653|- explicit user requests override both;
   654|- runtime may mechanically register raw payloads, logs, and tool outputs, but
   655|  should not invent semantic artifact meaning without a path contract,
   656|  commitment, or model/user declaration.
   657|
   658|Normal chat answers are not automatically artifacts. An output becomes expected
   659|only through a contract: user request, model commitment, worker output contract,
   660|or known run path.
   661|
   662|## Minimal v0 Storage
   663|
   664|Start with files:
   665|
   666|- `ledger.md`;
   667|- `raw/` for full source/tool payloads;
   668|- `artifact-manifest.md` or `artifact-manifest.json`;
   669|- `resume-packet.md`;
   670|- optional generated indexes later.
   671|
   672|The v0 model should privilege strict invariants over schema completeness.
   673|
   674|## Finalization Rule
   675|
   676|Vesta should not finish from memory of activity. It should finish from recorded
   677|state, with bounded trust rather than maximal suspicion.
   678|
   679|- high-materiality factual claims have evidence, are labeled as hypotheses, or
   680|  are explicitly accepted with low confidence;
   681|- expected artifacts exist or are marked missing;
   682|- contradictions are resolved or explicitly blocking;
   683|- worker failures/truncation are visible;
   684|- verification ran or has a skip reason;
   685|- next action is clear when work remains.
   686|
   687|The evidence rule is not "prove every sentence." Low-risk interpretation,
   688|planning judgment, ideation, prioritization, and ordinary model synthesis may
   689|stand without source-grade evidence when they are clearly not material factual
   690|claims. The runtime should prevent unsupported important claims, not turn every
   691|session into an endless research loop.
   692|
   693|For non-coding work, finalization is domain-neutral: check objective,
   694|commitments, artifacts, material claims, gaps, contradictions, worker state, and
   695|next action. Tests, diffs, and validators are required only when the task
   696|touches code, configuration, or risky operational behavior.
   697|
   698|## Repair UX
   699|
   700|Repair gates should be model-facing first. A failed broad read, missing ledger
   701|entry, or missing finalization fact should return a short tool/gate error that
   702|tells the model how to repair the state. The UI may show a compact activity
   703|note, but should not interrupt the user unless a repair fails repeatedly or a
   704|real human preference is needed.
   705|
   706|## Worker Acceptance
   707|
   708|The parent agent should not accept delegated work from a summary alone. Worker
   709|acceptance requires:
   710|
   711|- a `worker_state` entry;
   712|- output refs or artifacts;
   713|- declared gaps/failures;
   714|- a spot audit for high-materiality claims.
   715|
   716|This keeps worker orchestration inspectable and prevents silent delegation
   717|failure from looking like completed work.
   718|
   719|## Raw Retention And Privacy
   720|
   721|Raw outputs are retained locally inside the run by default because auditability
   722|is a product feature. Vesta should provide cleanup/purge controls and later TTL
   723|configuration. Model-facing excerpts should be redacted where appropriate; raw
   724|payloads may remain unredacted as audit material, but this must be visible and
   725|controllable.
   726|
   727|## Non-Code Workspace Semantics
   728|
   729|Workspace is not always a Git repository. For document, research, planning,
   730|ideation, or reflective runs, workspace means the current project/domain/person
   731|context. These runs still receive the same primitives: `run_id`, `run.md`,
   732|`ledger.md`, `raw/`, artifact manifest, and finalization state.
   733|
   734|## Validator Boundary
   735|
   736|Selective validation is a v0 contract, not a full v0 engine. The runtime should
   737|record when validation is expected and run cheap deterministic or high-risk
   738|checks where available. A full validator engine belongs after the ledger/eval
   739|foundation is proven.
   740|", "total_lines": 739, "file_size": 29593, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false, "_vesta_retrieval": {"mode": "disciplined", "broad": true, "broad_reasons": ["requested 250 lines > threshold 200", "file size 29593 bytes > threshold 20000"]}}

{"content": "     1|# Vesta Runtime PRD
     2|
     3|Date: 2026-05-16
     4|Status: Product design complete; ready for implementation planning
     5|
     6|Source decision artifacts:
     7|
     8|- `VESTA_PRODUCT_IDEA.md`
     9|- `VESTA_DESIGN_INTERVIEW.md`
    10|- `VESTA_LEDGER_DESIGN.md`
    11|
    12|This PRD captures the accepted product direction. It does not create
    13|implementation tasks, slices, or issues.
    14|
    15|## 1. Problem Statement
    16|
    17|Local agent runs can produce useful coding, research, planning, and synthesis
    18|work, but the harness determines whether the output is trustworthy. Today a run
    19|can appear successful while hiding weak evidence, missing artifacts, lost
    20|compaction state, broad context ingestion, unsupported claims, silent worker
    21|failure, or final answers based on memory rather than durable state.
    22|
    23|Vesta Runtime should make long-running local agent work inspectable. A run
    24|should leave durable evidence of what happened, what was known, what was
    25|decided, which artifacts exist, what failed, what remains uncertain, and what
    26|should happen next.
    27|
    28|## 2. Target Users
    29|
    30|1. Damyan, for local/private coding, research, planning, ideation, and
    31|   reflective work.
    32|2. Senior engineers and platform/infra users working on private codebases with
    33|   local or self-hosted model endpoints.
    34|3. Future small-team or enterprise users who need private agent capacity with
    35|   audit trails, after the personal/local loop is proven.
    36|
    37|The v0 product is personal/local first. Enterprise-compatible artifacts should
    38|exist from the beginning, but governance, multi-tenancy, auth, and deployment
    39|are later paths.
    40|
    41|## 3. Current Evidence And Research Signals
    42|
    43|1. Prior Hermes benchmark and audit research showed the need for durable run
    44|   state, grounded reports, artifact manifests, and better compaction/resume
    45|   behavior.
    46|2. Hermes already provides useful integration anchors: `session_id`,
    47|   `parent_session_id`, `task_id`, session storage, session lineage, tool
    48|   isolation, plugin hooks, tool-result persistence, delegation config, and
    49|   profile-aware storage.
    50|3. Hermes compaction rotates session identity, so Vesta needs a stable `run_id`
    51|   that survives session lineage changes.
    52|4. Hermes has mechanical large tool-result spill behavior. Vesta should
    53|   piggyback on that rather than duplicate raw-output retention.
    54|5. Hermes `read_file` has pagination, character guards, and repeated-read
    55|   dedup/blocking, but file reads still need a broader runtime retrieval
    56|   discipline for local hardware.
    57|6. Hermes has `hermes_time.py`; Vesta should reuse that clock path so models do
    58|   not author timestamps.
    59|7. Hermes delegation config already supports separate worker model/provider
    60|   lanes, which helps keep a dense main model's prompt cache hot while bounded
    61|   work runs elsewhere.
    62|8. Long-context research and practice support the direction: large documents
    63|   should not simply be shoved into context by default, but whole-document
    64|   reading remains valid when the task requires complete coverage.
    65|9. The accepted design phase defines Vesta as broader than a coding agent:
    66|   coding is the first hard eval surface, not the product boundary.
    67|
    68|## 4. Product Direction
    69|
    70|Vesta Runtime is a local/private agent runtime for auditable long-running work.
    71|It starts from a Hermes fork and initially stays aligned with Hermes where that
    72|reduces risk, but Vesta's product boundary is its own runtime discipline:
    73|
    74|- repeatable eval/run harness;
    75|- eager run identity and run directory;
    76|- Markdown-first durable ledger;
    77|- raw source/tool-output references;
    78|- ledger-aware compaction/resume;
    79|- retrieval discipline for local models;
    80|- whole-document mode for complete-coverage tasks;
    81|- artifact manifest and finalization gates;
    82|- worker state and acceptance discipline;
    83|- selective validation contract for high-risk work.
    84|
    85|The first useful surface is the run/eval substrate, not UI polish or ACP/AionUi.
    86|UI/TUI/CLI controls matter, but they should expose runtime state rather than
    87|define the product.
    88|
    89|## 5. Non-Goals / Out Of Scope
    90|
    91|1. No multi-tenant enterprise platform in v0.
    92|2. No full governance, auth, audit retention, or deployment system in v0.
    93|3. No user-facing task profiles for research/coding/planning/ideation in v0.
    94|4. No autonomous per-turn model router in v0.
    95|5. No JSONL/SQL as the model-authored ledger surface in v0.
    96|6. No full validator engine before the ledger/eval foundation is proven.
    97|7. No broad UI/control-plane build before runtime semantics are trustworthy.
    98|8. No dynamic prompt/tool-surface mutation to activate ledger behavior mid-run.
    99|9. No hidden semantic classifier deciding which raw outputs are important.
   100|10. No overfitting Vesta to coding at the expense of research, planning,
   101|    ideation, documents, and reflective work.
   102|
   103|## 6. User Stories
   104|
   105|1. As a local user, I want every Vesta run to have a durable `run_id` so that I
   106|   can inspect what happened after compaction, failure, or interruption.
   107|2. As a local user, I want Vesta to create run files at session start so that
   108|   recovery does not depend on whether the agent remembered to initialize
   109|   state later.
   110|3. As a local user, I want `ledger.md` to record what is known, decided,
   111|   missing, promised, and next so that conversation history is not the only
   112|   memory.
   113|4. As a local user, I want ledger entries to be Markdown so that I can read and
   114|   edit decisions without database tooling.
   115|5. As a local user, I want the runtime to add timestamps so that model guesses
   116|   do not corrupt chronology.
   117|6. As a local user, I want Vesta to preserve raw tool outputs outside prompt
   118|   context so that evidence remains inspectable without bloating the model
   119|   prompt.
   120|7. As a local user, I want large raw outputs to have stable refs and excerpts so
   121|   that reports can point to evidence without copying everything into the
   122|   ledger.
   123|8. As a local user, I want compaction to produce a resume packet from active
   124|   working state so that a resumed model knows the actual next action.
   125|9. As a local user, I want unfinished deliverables to survive compaction so that
   126|   the model cannot forget promised work.
   127|10. As a local user, I want final answers to be based on recorded state so that
   128|    the agent cannot finish from vague memory of activity.
   129|11. As a local user, I want artifact commitments tracked so that promised
   130|    reports, plans, patches, and handoffs do not silently disappear.
   131|12. As a local user, I want ordinary chat answers not to become artifacts unless
   132|    there is a contract so that the ledger does not become noisy.
   133|13. As a local user, I want material claims to have evidence or uncertainty
   134|    labels so that important conclusions are reviewable.
   135|14. As a local user, I want low-risk synthesis and ideation to remain fluid so
   136|    that the runtime does not turn every conversation into research bureaucracy.
   137|15. As a local user, I want retrieval to be locator-first by default so that
   138|    local models do not waste time ingesting unnecessary source content.
   139|16. As a local user, I want the model to be able to read whole documents when
   140|    complete coverage is actually needed.
   141|17. As a local user, I want whole-document processing to chunk large inputs and
   142|    write chunk findings to the ledger so that long documents can be understood
   143|    without relying on a single overlarge prompt.
   144|18. As a local user, I want chunk findings to keep raw source refs so that
   145|    important details can be reread instead of trusting summaries blindly.
   146|19. As a local user, I want retrieval strictness to be controllable so that I
   147|    can choose discipline or speed for the current run.
   148|20. As a local user, I want broad-read gates to repair missing context without
   149|    constantly interrupting me.
   150|21. As a local user, I want retrieval thresholds to be configurable so that
   151|    behavior can match my hardware and model context window.
   152|22. As a local user, I want non-code runs to have the same run/ledger/artifact
   153|    primitives as code runs so that research and planning are first-class.
   154|23. As a local user, I want workspaces to represent a project or domain, not
   155|    only a Git repo, so that document and planning runs are organized.
   156|24. As a local user, I want raw retention to be local by default so that audit
   157|    material stays on my machine.
   158|25. As a local user, I want cleanup or purge controls so that raw retained data
   159|    does not accumulate forever.
   160|26. As a local user, I want model-facing excerpts to be redacted where
   161|    appropriate so that secrets are not unnecessarily fed back into context.
   162|27. As a local user, I want worker delegations to record objective, model lane,
   163|    artifacts, failures, and gaps so that worker behavior is inspectable.
   164|28. As a local user, I want parent agents to spot-audit high-materiality worker
   165|    claims so that worker summaries are not accepted blindly.
   166|29. As a local user, I want failed commands and tests to become visible state so
   167|    that the final answer cannot hide them.
   168|30. As a local user, I want unresolved contradictions to block or qualify final
   169|    output so that conflicting evidence is not papered over.
   170|31. As a local user, I want copied-repo execution for coding evals so that
   171|    agent experiments do not damage the original workspace.
   172|32. As a local user, I want prompt, config, model metadata, diff, tests,
   173|    artifacts, ledger, and final verdict captured so that a run is repeatable
   174|    and reviewable.
   175|33. As a local user, I want Vesta to reuse Hermes storage and lifecycle
   176|    primitives where sensible so that upstream alignment remains practical.
   177|34. As a local user, I want Vesta to avoid broad core patches unless a lifecycle
   178|    boundary needs a hook so that migration risk stays bounded.
   179|35. As a local user, I want stable resident prompt/tool contracts so that local
   180|    prompt cache behavior is not broken by dynamic instructions.
   181|36. As a local user, I want repair failures to surface only when meaningful so
   182|    that the runtime does not feel slower than the model.
   183|37. As a future implementer, I want PRD-level decisions separated from task
   184|    slices so that implementation planning can proceed without reopening
   185|    product direction.
   186|38. As a future team user, I want Vesta's local artifacts to be compatible with
   187|    later audit and governance work even though v0 is personal/local first.
   188|39. As a future UI user, I want retrieval strictness and whole-document
   189|    thresholds exposed in UI/TUI/CLI so that runtime behavior is not hidden.
   190|40. As a future validator author, I want validator expectations recorded before
   191|    the full engine exists so that high-risk checks can grow incrementally.
   192|
   193|## 7. Functional Requirements
   194|
   195|### Run Substrate
   196|
   197|1. Vesta MUST create a stable `run_id` at Vesta session start.
   198|2. Vesta MUST create a run directory, `run.md`, and `ledger.md` immediately at
   199|   session start.
   200|3. Vesta MUST keep `run_id` separate from Hermes `session_id`.
   201|4. Vesta MUST record Hermes session lineage inside run state.
   202|5. Vesta SHOULD store run state under the active Hermes home using
   203|   `get_hermes_home()`.
   204|6. Vesta SHOULD group runs by deterministic normalized workspace hash.
   205|
   206|### Ledger
   207|
   208|1. Vesta MUST use Markdown as the model-facing ledger surface in v0.
   209|2. Vesta MUST provide a dedicated `ledger_append` primitive for normal ledger
   210|   writes.
   211|3. `ledger_append` MUST keep model-provided input small: `entry_type`, `title`,
   212|   `statement`, `refs`, `status`, `materiality`, and `next_action`.
   213|4. Runtime MUST add id, timestamp, session/run metadata, and actor.
   214|5. Runtime MUST own timestamps through `hermes_time.py` or a Vesta wrapper.
   215|6. Ledger entries SHOULD be append-only and small.
   216|7. Ledger state MUST distinguish claims, actions, artifacts, decisions, gaps,
   217|   contradictions, commitments, worker state, checkpoints, and next steps.
   218|
   219|### Raw Capture
   220|
   221|1. Vesta MUST not inline large raw outputs in `ledger.md`.
   222|2. Vesta SHOULD piggyback on Hermes tool-result persistence instead of copying
   223|   the retention pipeline.
   224|3. Vesta SHOULD route persisted outputs into the active run `raw/` directory.
   225|4. Vesta MUST record stable refs, excerpts, hashes where available, tool name,
   226|   arguments where appropriate, exit status where available, and capture time.
   227|5. Runtime MUST NOT add a hidden semantic classifier for raw-output importance.
   228|
   229|### Retrieval Discipline
   230|
   231|1. Vesta MUST default to locator-first retrieval for normal source work.
   232|2. The abstract retrieval rule MUST be: prefer the smallest source range that
   233|   can answer the task honestly.
   234|3. Full-document reading MUST remain allowed when complete coverage is needed.
   235|4. Complete coverage MUST be treated as a deliberate retrieval contract, not the
   236|   default source-reading path.
   237|5. In `disciplined` mode, broad reads MUST repair only when locator history,
   238|   complete-coverage need, and model-declared escalation reason are all absent.
   239|6. In `permissive` mode, broad reads SHOULD proceed with lightweight ledger
   240|   reason and raw refs.
   241|7. v0 retrieval strictness MUST be two-state: `disciplined` and `permissive`.
   242|8. v0 MUST NOT include an `off` mode for retrieval discipline.
   243|9. Broad-read detection SHOULD consider line count, file size, token estimate,
   244|   whole-file requests, repeated adjacent reads, and post-compaction rereads.
   245|10. Broad-read thresholds MUST be configurable, not hidden constants.
   246|
   247|### Whole-Document Mode
   248|
   249|1. Whole-document mode MUST estimate token or size before ingestion.
   250|2. If an artifact exceeds the configured threshold, Vesta MUST use
   251|   chunk/ledger processing.
   252|3. Chunking SHOULD prefer document structure when available and fall back to
   253|   size-based chunking.
   254|4. Each chunk SHOULD produce a ledger finding with source refs and unresolved
   255|   gaps.
   256|5. Each chunk after the first SHOULD receive a compact rolling recap of prior
   257|   chunks, unresolved terms/questions, and the document objective as working
   258|   context.
   259|6. The rolling recap SHOULD be updated after each chunk and collapsed
   260|   hierarchically if it grows too large.
   261|7. Final synthesis SHOULD read from accumulated ledger findings.
   262|8. High-materiality details SHOULD trigger targeted reread of the smallest raw
   263|   range.
   264|9. Whole-document thresholds MUST be user-controllable through UI/TUI/CLI or
   265|   config. Until UI exists, config/CLI exposure is acceptable and missing UI is
   266|   documented product debt.
   267|
   268|### Compaction / Resume
   269|
   270|1. Compaction MUST write a checkpoint entry.
   271|2. Compaction MUST build a resume packet from active working state, not prose
   272|   transcript summary.
   273|3. Resume packet MUST include objective, phase, commitments, decisions, verified
   274|   claims, open gaps, contradictions, worker status, artifact manifest, ledger
   275|   path, and exactly one next action.
   276|4. If a deliverable is unfinished, `next_action` MUST NOT be `None`.
   277|5. After compaction, the first material action MUST consult resume packet and
   278|   `ledger.md` status.
   279|6. If no material state exists, Vesta SHOULD avoid ceremony.
   280|
   281|### Gates And Finalization
   282|
   283|1. Hard gates MUST use repair turns first.
   284|2. Runtime MUST NOT silently auto-append semantic ledger entries.
   285|3. Core gates SHOULD apply before compaction, before final response, after
   286|   explicit user decision, and after artifact write.
   287|4. Quality gates SHOULD apply after failed command/test, worker completion or
   288|   failure, unresolved contradiction, and unsupported high-materiality claim.
   289|5. Finalization MUST use bounded trust.
   290|6. Finalization MUST check objective, commitments, artifacts, material claims,
   291|   gaps, contradictions, worker state, verification or skip reason, and next
   292|   action.
   293|7. Non-code finalization MUST NOT require tests, diffs, or validators unless
   294|   the task touches code, configuration, or risky operational behavior.
   295|
   296|### Artifacts
   297|
   298|1. Expected artifacts MUST be created only by user request, model commitment,
   299|   worker contract, or known run path.
   300|2. Normal chat answers MUST NOT automatically become artifacts.
   301|3. Artifact tracking MUST be domain-neutral across reports, plans, decision
   302|   memos, prompts, handoffs, patches, eval results, generated documents, and
   303|   saved syntheses.
   304|
   305|### Workers
   306|
   307|1. v0 worker spawning MUST be explicit or config-driven, not autonomous routing.
   308|2. Vesta SHOULD reuse Hermes `delegation.*` configuration first.
   309|3. Worker state MUST record assigned objective, output contract, model/provider
   310|   class without secrets, artifacts, failures, gaps, and next action.
   311|4. Parent acceptance MUST require worker state, output refs or artifacts,
   312|   declared gaps/failures, and spot audit for high-materiality claims.
   313|
   314|### Raw Retention And Privacy
   315|
   316|1. Raw outputs SHOULD be retained locally inside the run by default.
   317|2. Vesta SHOULD provide cleanup/purge controls.
   318|3. Vesta SHOULD support future TTL configuration.
   319|4. Model-facing excerpts SHOULD be redacted where appropriate.
   320|5. Raw payloads MAY remain unredacted as audit material, but this must be
   321|   visible and controllable.
   322|
   323|### Validator Boundary
   324|
   325|1. v0 MUST record the selective validator contract.
   326|2. v0 SHOULD run cheap deterministic or high-risk checks where available.
   327|3. A full validator engine is out of scope until the ledger/eval foundation is
   328|   proven.
   329|
   330|## 8. UX Requirements
   331|
   332|1. The user should not have to choose research/coding/planning profiles.
   333|2. Retrieval strictness should be visible and controllable in UI/TUI/CLI, or
   334|   config until UI exists.
   335|3. Whole-document threshold should be visible and controllable in UI/TUI/CLI,
   336|   or config until UI exists.
   337|4. Repair gates should be model-facing first.
   338|5. User interruption should happen only after repeated repair failure or a real
   339|   preference choice.
   340|6. UI activity notes should be compact and explain what gate fired without
   341|   turning every repair into a user-facing prompt.
   342|7. Prompt/tool surface should remain stable during a run to preserve local
   343|   prompt-cache behavior.
   344|8. Run artifacts should be easy to inspect from disk.
   345|9. User-facing paths should use profile-aware display helpers.
   346|10. Vesta should feel like a runtime assistant, not a form-heavy workflow tool.
   347|
   348|## 9. Data / Integration Requirements
   349|
   350|1. Use Hermes `get_hermes_home()` as the v0 root resolver.
   351|2. Use Hermes `display_hermes_home()` for user-facing path display where
   352|   applicable.
   353|3. Reuse or wrap Hermes `hermes_time.py` / `hermes_time.now()` for all runtime
   354|   timestamps.
   355|4. Anchor Vesta runs to Hermes `session_id` and `task_id`, while preserving a
   356|   separate `run_id`.
   357|5. Record Hermes `parent_session_id` lineage for compaction continuation.
   358|6. Reuse Hermes plugin hooks where sufficient: `on_session_start`,
   359|   `on_session_end`, `pre_tool_call`, `post_tool_call`, `pre_llm_call`,
   360|   `post_llm_call`.
   361|7. Add narrow lifecycle hooks only where Hermes lacks a clean boundary,
   362|   especially compaction/resume.
   363|8. Piggyback on Hermes `maybe_persist_tool_result` and `BudgetConfig` for raw
   364|   tool-output persistence.
   365|9. Reuse Hermes `delegation.*` config for worker model lanes.
   366|10. Keep secrets out of worker state and run metadata.
   367|11. Store Vesta run state outside project repos by default.
   368|12. Recommended storage shape:
   369|
   370|```text
   371|{get_hermes_home()}/vesta/
   372|  workspaces/
   373|    <workspace_hash>/
   374|      workspace.md
   375|      runs/
   376|        <run_id>/
   377|          run.md
   378|          ledger.md
   379|          resume-packet.md
   380|          artifact-manifest.md
   381|          raw/
   382|          finalization.md
   383|```
   384|
   385|## 10. Implementation Notes Already Decided
   386|
   387|1. Plugin-first with minimal core hooks.
   388|2. Keep Vesta state in files for v0.
   389|3. Do not overload Hermes SQLite sessions as the Vesta run ledger.
   390|4. Create run seed files eagerly.
   391|5. Keep ledger behavior resident and stable from session start.
   392|6. Do not dynamically inject ledger instructions every few turns.
   393|7. Do not make user-facing profiles.
   394|8. Do not implement automatic worker/model routing in v0.
   395|9. Use `disciplined` retrieval as default.
   396|10. Use `permissive` as the v0 escape hatch, not `off`.
   397|11. Use repair-first gates.
   398|12. Store dynamic state in `run.md` and `ledger.md`, not in changing prompt
   399|    prefixes.
   400|13. Treat Hermes alignment as implementation aid, not product veto.
   401|14. Keep naming migration flexible; Vesta may rename Hermes primitives after
   402|    behavior is proven.
   403|
   404|## 11. Open Questions
   405|
   406|1. What are the initial default values for broad-read line, byte, and token
   407|   thresholds?
   408|2. Which tokenizer or estimator should be used for local Qwen/Llama-style
   409|   models?
   410|3. What exact config keys or CLI commands expose retrieval strictness and
   411|   whole-document thresholds before UI/TUI support exists?
   412|4. What is the exact minimal compaction/resume hook API in Hermes?
   413|5. How should workspace hashes be normalized for non-code/domain work?
   414|6. What is the first cleanup/purge UX for retained raw payloads?
   415|7. Which raw payload classes should be redacted before model-facing excerpts?
   416|8. How deep should parent spot-audit be for worker high-materiality claims?
   417|9. Which deterministic validator checks belong in the first v0 contract?
   418|10. What exact artifact-manifest format should v0 use: Markdown only or a small
   419|    generated JSON index beside Markdown?
   420|11. How should Vesta expose current retrieval policy to the model without prompt
   421|    churn?
   422|12. How should Vesta represent document structure when chunking PDFs, Markdown,
   423|    source files, and plain text?
   424|
   425|## 12. Testing / QA Decisions
   426|
   427|1. The first QA surface is repeatable eval/run harness behavior.
   428|2. Tests should cover copied-repo run creation for coding tasks.
   429|3. Tests should cover run state creation at session start.
   430|4. Tests should cover runtime-owned timestamps.
   431|5. Tests should cover `ledger_append` formatting and append-only behavior.
   432|6. Tests should cover compaction checkpoint and resume packet generation.
   433|7. Tests should cover `run_id` survival across Hermes session rotation.
   434|8. Tests should cover raw-output spill into run `raw/` storage.
   435|9. Tests should cover broad-read gate behavior in `disciplined` mode.
   436|10. Tests should cover permissive mode reducing broad-read repair.
   437|11. Tests should cover whole-document chunk/ledger processing.
   438|12. Tests should cover rolling recap reinjection for later chunks in a long
   439|    document.
   440|13. Tests should cover finalization with supported and unsupported
   441|    high-materiality claims.
   442|14. Tests should cover finalization for non-code work without requiring tests or
   443|    diffs.
   444|15. Tests should cover worker state recording and parent acceptance checks.
   445|16. Tests should cover prompt-cache-sensitive behavior by verifying stable
   446|    prompt/tool surface across runtime policy changes.
   447|17. Manual QA should inspect generated run directories and ledger readability.
   448|18. Regression tests should ensure Vesta does not write run state into project
   449|    repos by default.
   450|
   451|## 13. Definition Of Done
   452|
   453|The v0 PRD is satisfied when a Vesta-enabled local run can:
   454|
   455|1. create stable run identity and seed files at session start;
   456|2. keep a Markdown ledger updated through `ledger_append`;
   457|3. preserve raw source/tool-output refs outside prompt context;
   458|4. survive compaction with a ledger checkpoint and resume packet;
   459|5. enforce retrieval discipline with `disciplined` and `permissive` modes;
   460|6. process large complete-coverage documents through chunk/ledger workflow;
   461|7. carry prior chunk recaps forward when processing later chunks;
   462|8. keep whole-document thresholds configurable;
   463|9. track declared artifacts and missing expected outputs;
   464|10. record worker state and require parent acceptance for material worker output;
   465|11. finalize from recorded state instead of conversation memory;
   466|12. support non-code runs with the same workspace/run/ledger primitives;
   467|13. run cheap deterministic/high-risk validator checks where available;
   468|14. provide enough captured state for a human to inspect prompt/config/model
   469|    metadata, ledger, raw refs, artifacts, failures, verification, and final
   470|    verdict;
   471|15. preserve local prompt-cache stability by avoiding dynamic prompt/tool
   472|    surface mutation.
   473|
   474|Design is complete. Implementation planning should decompose this PRD into
   475|ordered local prototype slices without reopening accepted product semantics by
   476|default.

{"content": "     1|"""File-backed Vesta run state.
     2|
     3|This module deliberately avoids becoming a database. The model-facing runtime
     4|surface is Markdown, with small helper functions that own ids, timestamps, and
     5|run/session metadata.
     6|"""
     7|
     8|from __future__ import annotations
     9|
    10|from contextvars import ContextVar
    11|from dataclasses import dataclass
    12|from pathlib import Path
    13|from typing import Any
    14|import hashlib
    15|import json
    16|import os
    17|import re
    18|import threading
    19|import uuid
    20|
    21|from hermes_constants import get_hermes_home
    22|from hermes_time import now as hermes_now
    23|
    24|
    25|_CURRENT_RUN: ContextVar["VestaRun | None"] = ContextVar("vesta_current_run", default=None)
    26|_STATE_LOCK = threading.RLock()
    27|
    28|VALID_LEDGER_ENTRY_TYPES = {
    29|    "claim",
    30|    "decision",
    31|    "action",
    32|    "gap",
    33|    "contradiction",
    34|    "commitment",
    35|    "artifact",
    36|    "worker_state",
    37|    "checkpoint",
    38|    "next_step",
    39|    "failure",
    40|    "source_ref",
    41|    "raw_ref",
    42|    "verification",
    43|    "document_chunk_finding",
    44|    "document_recap",
    45|}
    46|
    47|VALID_MATERIALITY = {"low", "medium", "high", "critical"}
    48|
    49|
    50|@dataclass(frozen=True)
    51|class VestaRun:
    52|    """Resolved Vesta run paths and identity."""
    53|
    54|    run_id: str
    55|    workspace_hash: str
    56|    workspace_path: str
    57|    run_dir: Path
    58|    run_md_path: Path
    59|    ledger_path: Path
    60|    resume_packet_path: Path
    61|    artifact_manifest_path: Path
    62|    finalization_path: Path
    63|    worker_state_path: Path
    64|    validator_result_path: Path
    65|    control_plane_path: Path
    66|    handoff_path: Path
    67|    raw_dir: Path
    68|    raw_index_path: Path
    69|    created_at: str
    70|
    71|
    72|def _timestamp() -> str:
    73|    return hermes_now().isoformat()
    74|
    75|
    76|def _slug(value: str, *, max_len: int = 48) -> str:
    77|    cleaned = re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_.-]+", "-", value.strip()).strip("-._")
    78|    if not cleaned:
    79|        cleaned = "workspace"
    80|    return cleaned[:max_len]
    81|
    82|
    83|def _workspace_path(workspace_path: str | os.PathLike[str] | None = None) -> str:
    84|    raw = (
    85|        str(workspace_path)
    86|        if workspace_path is not None
    87|        else os.getenv("TERMINAL_CWD") or os.getcwd()
    88|    )
    89|    return str(Path(raw).expanduser().resolve())
    90|
    91|
    92|def _workspace_hash(workspace_path: str) -> str:
    93|    digest = hashlib.sha256(workspace_path.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:16]
    94|    return f"{_slug(Path(workspace_path).name)}-{digest}"
    95|
    96|
    97|def _new_run_id() -> str:
    98|    ts = hermes_now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    99|    return f"run_{ts}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
   100|
   101|
   102|def _write_if_missing(path: Path, content: str) -> None:
   103|    if path.exists():
   104|        return
   105|    path.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
   106|
   107|
   108|def _set_env_for_run(run: VestaRun) -> None:
   109|    os.environ["VESTA_RUN_ID"] = run.run_id
   110|    os.environ["VESTA_RUN_DIR"] = str(run.run_dir)
   111|    os.environ["VESTA_LEDGER_PATH"] = str(run.ledger_path)
   112|
   113|
   114|def set_current_run(run: VestaRun | None) -> None:
   115|    """Set the current run for this execution context."""
   116|
   117|    _CURRENT_RUN.set(run)
   118|    if run is not None:
   119|        _set_env_for_run(run)
   120|    else:
   121|        for key in ("VESTA_RUN_ID", "VESTA_RUN_DIR", "VESTA_LEDGER_PATH"):
   122|            os.environ.pop(key, None)
   123|
   124|
   125|def get_current_run() -> VestaRun | None:
   126|    """Return the active Vesta run, if one is bound."""
   127|
   128|    return _CURRENT_RUN.get()
   129|
   130|
   131|def _run_from_env() -> VestaRun | None:
   132|    run_id = os.getenv("VESTA_RUN_ID")
   133|    run_dir_raw = os.getenv("VESTA_RUN_DIR")
   134|    ledger_raw = os.getenv("VESTA_LEDGER_PATH")
   135|    if not run_id or not run_dir_raw:
   136|        return None
   137|    run_dir = Path(run_dir_raw)
   138|    ledger_path = Path(ledger_raw) if ledger_raw else run_dir / "ledger.md"
   139|    workspace_path = os.getenv("VESTA_WORKSPACE_PATH") or ""
   140|    workspace_hash = os.getenv("VESTA_WORKSPACE_HASH") or ""
   141|    run = VestaRun(
   142|        run_id=run_id,
   143|        workspace_hash=workspace_hash,
   144|        workspace_path=workspace_path,
   145|        run_dir=run_dir,
   146|        run_md_path=run_dir / "run.md",
   147|        ledger_path=ledger_path,
   148|        resume_packet_path=run_dir / "resume-packet.md",
   149|        artifact_manifest_path=run_dir / "artifact-manifest.md",
   150|        finalization_path=run_dir / "finalization.md",
   151|        worker_state_path=run_dir / "worker-state.md",
   152|        validator_result_path=run_dir / "validator-result.md",
   153|        control_plane_path=run_dir / "control-plane.md",
   154|        handoff_path=run_dir / "handoff.md",
   155|        raw_dir=run_dir / "raw",
   156|        raw_index_path=run_dir / "raw" / "index.md",
   157|        created_at="",
   158|    )
   159|    if run.run_dir.exists():
   160|        set_current_run(run)
   161|        return run
   162|    return None
   163|
   164|
   165|def create_run(
   166|    *,
   167|    session_id: str,
   168|    parent_session_id: str | None = None,
   169|    task_id: str | None = None,
   170|    workspace_path: str | os.PathLike[str] | None = None,
   171|    model: str | None = None,
   172|    provider: str | None = None,
   173|    platform: str | None = None,
   174|    run_id: str | None = None,
   175|) -> VestaRun:
   176|    """Create and bind a Vesta run with eager Markdown seed files."""
   177|
   178|    resolved_workspace = _workspace_path(workspace_path)
   179|    workspace_hash = _workspace_hash(resolved_workspace)
   180|    created_at = _timestamp()
   181|    rid = run_id or _new_run_id()
   182|    run_dir = get_hermes_home() / "vesta" / "workspaces" / workspace_hash / "runs" / rid
   183|    raw_dir = run_dir / "raw"
   184|    raw_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   185|
   186|    run = VestaRun(
   187|        run_id=rid,
   188|        workspace_hash=workspace_hash,
   189|        workspace_path=resolved_workspace,
   190|        run_dir=run_dir,
   191|        run_md_path=run_dir / "run.md",
   192|        ledger_path=run_dir / "ledger.md",
   193|        resume_packet_path=run_dir / "resume-packet.md",
   194|        artifact_manifest_path=run_dir / "artifact-manifest.md",
   195|        finalization_path=run_dir / "finalization.md",
   196|        worker_state_path=run_dir / "worker-state.md",
   197|        validator_result_path=run_dir / "validator-result.md",
   198|        control_plane_path=run_dir / "control-plane.md",
   199|        handoff_path=run_dir / "handoff.md",
   200|        raw_dir=raw_dir,
   201|        raw_index_path=raw_dir / "index.md",
   202|        created_at=created_at,
   203|    )
   204|
   205|    _write_if_missing(run.run_md_path, _render_run_seed(
   206|        run=run,
   207|        session_id=session_id,
   208|        parent_session_id=parent_session_id,
   209|        task_id=task_id,
   210|        model=model,
   211|        provider=provider,
   212|        platform=platform,
   213|    ))
   214|    _write_if_missing(run.ledger_path, _render_ledger_seed(run, session_id=session_id))
   215|    _write_if_missing(run.raw_index_path, _render_raw_index_seed(run))
   216|    _write_if_missing(run.artifact_manifest_path, _render_artifact_manifest_seed(run))
   217|    _write_if_missing(run.worker_state_path, _render_worker_state_seed(run))
   218|    _write_if_missing(run.validator_result_path, _render_validator_result_seed(run))
   219|    _write_if_missing(run.control_plane_path, _render_control_plane_seed(run))
   220|    _write_if_missing(run.handoff_path, _render_handoff_seed(run))
   221|
   222|    os.environ["VESTA_WORKSPACE_PATH"] = run.workspace_path
   223|    os.environ["VESTA_WORKSPACE_HASH"] = run.workspace_hash
   224|    set_current_run(run)
   225|    return run
   226|
   227|
   228|def ensure_current_run(*, session_id: str | None = None) -> VestaRun:
   229|    """Return active run or create a minimal one when a Hermes session exists."""
   230|
   231|    run = get_current_run() or _run_from_env()
   232|    if run is not None:
   233|        return run
   234|    sid = session_id or os.getenv("HERMES_SESSION_ID")
   235|    if not sid:
   236|        raise RuntimeError("No active Vesta run or Hermes session is available")
   237|    return create_run(session_id=sid)
   238|
   239|
   240|def _render_run_seed(
   241|    *,
   242|    run: VestaRun,
   243|    session_id: str,
   244|    parent_session_id: str | None,
   245|    task_id: str | None,
   246|    model: str | None,
   247|    provider: str | None,
   248|    platform: str | None,
   249|) -> str:
   250|    parent = parent_session_id or ""
   251|    lineage = f"- {session_id}"

     2|
     3|from __future__ import annotations
     4|
     5|from pathlib import Path
     6|from typing import Any
     7|import difflib
     8|import hashlib
     9|import json
    10|import os
    11|import shutil
    12|import uuid
    13|
    14|from agent.redact import redact_sensitive_text
    15|
    16|from .state import (
    17|    append_ledger_entry,
    18|    capture_raw_output,
    19|    ensure_current_run,
    20|    record_artifact,
    21|)
    22|
    23|
    24|DEFAULT_EXCLUDED_PATHS = [
    25|    ".git",
    26|    ".hg",
    27|    ".svn",
    28|    ".env",
    29|    ".env.local",
    30|    ".venv",
    31|    "venv",
    32|    "node_modules",
    33|    ".mypy_cache",
    34|    ".pytest_cache",
    35|    "__pycache__",
    36|    ".worktrees",
    37|    ".hermes",
    38|    "dist",
    39|    "build",
    40|]
    41|
    42|
    43|def _new_eval_id() -> str:
    44|    return f"eval_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:10]}"
    45|
    46|
    47|def _json_compact(value: Any) -> str:
    48|    return json.dumps(value, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True)
    49|
    50|
    51|def _redact_payload(value: Any) -> Any:
    52|    if isinstance(value, dict):
    53|        return {
    54|            str(key): "<redacted>" if _looks_secret_key(str(key)) else _redact_payload(item)
    55|            for key, item in value.items()
    56|        }
    57|    if isinstance(value, list):
    58|        return [_redact_payload(item) for item in value]
    59|    if isinstance(value, str):
    60|        return redact_sensitive_text(value)
    61|    return value
    62|
    63|
    64|def _looks_secret_key(key: str) -> bool:
    65|    lowered = key.lower()
    66|    return any(marker in lowered for marker in ("api_key", "apikey", "token", "secret", "password"))
    67|
    68|
    69|def _path_is_within(path: Path, root: Path) -> bool:
    70|    try:
    71|        path.resolve(strict=False).relative_to(root.resolve(strict=False))
    72|        return True
    73|    except ValueError:
    74|        return False
    75|
    76|
    77|def _normalize_exclusions(
    78|    excluded_paths: list[str] | None,
    79|    *,
    80|    include_sensitive_paths: bool,
    81|) -> list[str]:
    82|    exclusions = [] if include_sensitive_paths else list(DEFAULT_EXCLUDED_PATHS)
    83|    exclusions.extend(path for path in (excluded_paths or []) if path)
    84|    seen: set[str] = set()
    85|    unique: list[str] = []
    86|    for path in exclusions:
    87|        norm = path.strip().strip("/")
    88|        if norm and norm not in seen:
    89|            unique.append(norm)
    90|            seen.add(norm)
    91|    return unique
    92|
    93|
    94|def _copy_ignore(excluded_paths: list[str]):
    95|    excluded_names = {path for path in excluded_paths if "/" not in path}
    96|    excluded_rel = {Path(path) for path in excluded_paths if "/" in path}
    97|
    98|    def ignore(src: str, names: list[str]) -> set[str]:
    99|        src_path = Path(src)
   100|        ignored: set[str] = set()
   101|        for name in names:
   102|            if name in excluded_names:
   103|                ignored.add(name)
   104|                continue
   105|            candidate = src_path / name
   106|            for rel in excluded_rel:
   107|                if candidate.match(str(rel)) or name == rel.name:
   108|                    ignored.add(name)
   109|                    break
   110|        return ignored
   111|
   112|    return ignore
   113|
   114|
   115|def start_coding_eval(
   116|    *,
   117|    original_workspace: str,
   118|    prompt: str,
   119|    model: str = "",
   120|    provider: str = "",
   121|    config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
   122|    excluded_paths: list[str] | None = None,
   123|    include_sensitive_paths: bool = False,
   124|    session_id: str | None = None,
   125|) -> dict[str, Any]:
   126|    """Create an isolated copied workspace and record eval seed state."""
   127|
   128|    run = ensure_current_run(session_id=session_id)
   129|    original = Path(original_workspace).expanduser().resolve()
   130|    if not original.exists() or not original.is_dir():
   131|        raise ValueError(f"original_workspace must be an existing directory: {original_workspace}")
   132|
   133|    eval_id = _new_eval_id()
   134|    eval_dir = run.run_dir / "evals" / eval_id
   135|    eval_workspace = eval_dir / "workspace"
   136|    if _path_is_within(eval_workspace, original):
   137|        raise ValueError("eval workspace must not be inside original workspace")
   138|    eval_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=False)
   139|
   140|    exclusions = _normalize_exclusions(excluded_paths, include_sensitive_paths=include_sensitive_paths)
   141|    shutil.copytree(original, eval_workspace, ignore=_copy_ignore(exclusions), symlinks=True)
   142|
   143|    prompt_ref = capture_raw_output(

   154|        "excluded_paths": exclusions,
   155|    })
   156|    config_ref = capture_raw_output(
   157|        content=_json_compact(safe_config),

   159|        tool_use_id=f"{eval_id}_config",
   160|        session_id=session_id,
   161|    )

   164|    eval_md.write_text(
   165|        f"""# Vesta Coding Eval
   166|
   167|Eval ID: `{eval_id}`

   169|Eval Workspace: `{eval_workspace}`
   170|Prompt Ref: `{prompt_ref['raw_ref']}`
   171|Config Ref: `{config_ref['raw_ref']}`
   172|Model: `{redact_sensitive_text(model)}`
   173|Provider: `{redact_sensitive_text(provider)}`
   174|Included Sensitive Paths: `{include_sensitive_paths}`
   175|Excluded Paths: `{', '.join(exclusions)}`
   176|Final Verdict: `pending`
   177|
   178|## Verification
   179|
   180|- pending
   181|
   182|## Diff
   183|
   184|- pending
   185|""",
   186|        encoding="utf-8",
   187|    )

   189|    record_artifact(
   190|        path=str(eval_md),
   191|        artifact_type="eval",
   192|        expected_by="run_path",
   193|        status="exists",
   194|        session_id=session_id,
   195|    )
   196|    append_ledger_entry(
   197|        entry_type="action",
   198|        title="Coding eval workspace created",
   199|        statement=f"Copied `{original}` to isolated eval workspace `{eval_workspace}`.",
   200|        refs=[str(eval_md), prompt_ref["raw_ref"], config_ref["raw_ref"]],
   201|        status="active",
   202|        materiality="high",
   203|        session_id=session_id,
   204|        actor="runtime",
   205|        structured_payload={
   206|            "eval_id": eval_id,
   207|            "original_workspace": str(original),
   208|            "eval_workspace": str(eval_workspace),
   209|            "excluded_paths": exclusions,
   210|        },
